back_translation: In the city of Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, a centurion of a military regiment called the Italian Regiment.
He was a man devout in religion and fearing God, together with all his household. He gave alms generously to the Jewish people and prayed to God continually.
One day at about three o'clock in the afternoon, Cornelius saw a vision clearly — an angel of God came to him and said, "Cornelius."
Cornelius stared at the angel in fear and asked, "Sir, what is it?" The angel answered him, "Your prayers and your alms have gone up as a memorial before God.
Now send men to the city of Joppa to summon a certain Simon whose nickname is Peter.
He is staying with a certain Simon, a tanner, whose house is located by the sea."
When the angel who spoke to him had gone, Cornelius called two of his servants and a soldier who was devoted to God from among his close attendants.
He related the whole story to them and sent them to the city of Joppa.
The next day, while they were still traveling and drawing near to the city, Peter went up on the rooftop to pray at about noon.
Peter began to be hungry and wanted to eat, but while the people of the house were preparing food, he entered into a trance.
Peter saw heaven open, and a certain vessel like a large linen sheet was being let down to the earth by its four corners.
In that vessel were four-footed animals, crawling creatures of the earth, and birds of the air of every kind.
Then there came a voice speaking to him, "Peter, get up, kill and eat."
But Peter said respectfully, "O Lord, that is impossible, because I have never eaten anything defiled or impure."
The voice spoke to him a second time, "Whatever God has cleansed, you must not call defiled."
This event happened three times, and then immediately the vessel was taken back up into heaven.

While Peter was puzzling in his mind about what the vision he had seen meant, just at that moment the men whom Cornelius had sent inquired their way to Simon's house and came and stood at the compound gate.
They called out and asked whether Simon nicknamed Peter was staying there.
While Peter was still pondering the vision, the Spirit said to him, "Behold, several men are looking for you.
Get up, go downstairs, and go with them without any hesitation, because I am the one who sent them."
So Peter went down to those men and said, "Behold, I am the one you are looking for. What business brings you here?"

They replied, "Cornelius the centurion, a righteous man and one who fears God, who is respected among the entire Jewish nation, received instruction from a holy angel to summon you to his house and to listen to words from you."
So Peter invited them in and hosted them as visiting guests. The next day Peter prepared himself and set out on the journey with them, and some of the brothers from the city of Joppa went along.
The following day Peter entered the city of Caesarea. Cornelius had already invited his relatives and close friends and was waiting to welcome Peter and his group.
As Peter was about to enter, Cornelius came out to meet him and sank down at Peter's feet to worship.
But Peter helped him up and said, "Please stand up, I myself am only a man."
While conversing with Cornelius, Peter went into the house and found that many people had gathered there.
Peter said to them, "You all know that it is against custom for a Jew to associate closely or to visit with a foreigner. But God has shown me that I should not call anyone defiled or impure.
For this reason, when you invited me, I came without any objection. So I ask, for what reason did you invite me?"
So Cornelius answered, "Four days ago at this same time, I was praying at three in the afternoon at my house, and behold, a certain man wearing radiant clothing came and stood before me,
and said, 'Cornelius, your prayer has been heard, and your alms are a memorial before God.
Therefore send to the city of Joppa and invite Simon nicknamed Peter to come. He is staying at the house of Simon the tanner, which is located by the sea.'
So I sent men to you immediately, and you have kindly come. Now then, all of us have gathered in the presence of God to hear everything that the Lord has commanded you."
Peter then began to speak, saying, "Now I truly understand that God does not show favoritism,
but in every nation, anyone who fears Him and does what is righteous — that person is accepted by Him.
He sent his word to the nation of Israel, proclaiming the gospel of peace through Jesus Christ, who is the Lord over all things.
You yourselves already know the events that happened throughout the region of Judea, which began from the region of Galilee after the baptism that John proclaimed.

That is, concerning Jesus of Nazareth, whom God anointed with the Holy Spirit and with power; he went about doing good deeds and healing all the people who were oppressed by the devil, because God was with him.
And we are witnesses of everything he did, both in the land of the Jews and in Jerusalem. They killed him by hanging him on a tree.
On the third day God raised him up from the dead and granted that he should manifest himself visibly,
not to all the people, but to witnesses whom God had chosen beforehand — to us, who ate and drank with him after he had risen from the dead.
He commanded us to proclaim to the people and to bear witness that he is the one whom God has appointed to be judge of the living and the dead.
All the prophets bear witness about him that everyone who believes in him will receive forgiveness of sins through his name."
While Peter was speaking these words, the Holy Spirit came down upon all those who were listening to the word.
The circumcised believers who had come with Peter were amazed, because the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out also upon the Gentiles.
For they heard them speaking various languages and praising God. Peter then said,
"Since they have received the Holy Spirit just as we have, can anyone forbid these people from receiving baptism with water?"
Then Peter commanded that they receive baptism in the name of Jesus Christ. After that, they requested that Peter stay there for several days.
